commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=33c013ed8d2bdb030cb02fd8561c04d7894ccaad)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a delayed `web_fetch` progress emit, allow-lists `web_fetch` non-exec partial results as item `progressText`, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +99, Tests +464. Total +563 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: start `web_fetch` with a URL, emit the new update, then format the item event; the existing `meta` value wins before `progressText`.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed to fix the channel-rendering gap and add real channel-visible proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's proof environment.

target.title: fix(web-fetch): surface progress during long fetches
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

`web_fetch` produces a fully silent gap between agent invocation and tool result during slow TTFB or slow body streams. Channel UIs render that gap as dead air — a visible source of friction for slow-TTFB and slow-body-stream fetches.

Why does this matter now?

`progressText` is already the canonical channel-visible progress field, plumbed through `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts:buildChannelProgressDraftLine` (the `meta ?? summary ?? … ?? progressText` fallback). Today only `exec` / `bash` populate it (on the sibling `kind:"command"` item). Adding a single allow-listed non-exec tool — `web_fetch` — reuses the same field on the existing `kind:"tool"` item with no channel-adapter changes.

What is the intended outcome?

Channel users see a single generic "Fetching web page…" line after ~1 s when the network work has not returned. Fast fetches stay silent. Fetch result, error type, cache, timeout, abort, retry, and SSRF guard are unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Per-tool progress for other built-in tools (browser, image_generate, video_generate, mcp).
- MCP `notifications/progress` plumbing.
- Generalized non-exec rate-limit redesign or progress-infrastructure refactor.
- Channel renderer changes: `channel-streaming.ts` and every `extensions/*` adapter is untouched.
- Adding non-`web_fetch` tools to the runtime allow-list.

What does success look like?

- A single delayed progress event reaches the existing channel progress draft path for slow fetches.
- Fast fetches emit zero progress events.
- The emit text is a fixed literal — no URL, query, header, cookie, body, or tool argument.
- Exec/bash `progressText` flow is unchanged.
- Non-allow-listed tools' `partialResult` continues to flow on the upstream `stream:"tool"` channel only (ACP/TUI/gateway), never reaching a channel draft.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ts`: the outer `try/finally` around the existing network/extract body releases the timer exactly once across every exit path (success, provider-fallback return, fetch-error rethrow, body-read throw). `emitWebFetchProgressSafely` swallows a throwing `onUpdate`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`: `NON_EXEC_PROGRESS_SAFE_TOOLS = Set("web_fetch")` is the contract boundary. Adding a tool requires auditing its `onUpdate` callsite for URL/body/header leakage. `extractLiveNonExecProgressText` reuses the existing 8000-char cap.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No upstream issue. Driven by a candidate selection report from masato-local UX-perf evaluation (web_fetch silent-gap reproduction with controlled slow HTTP harness).

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

None directly.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No specific maintainer request. Aligned with the existing `progressText` channel-rendering path already shipped for exec/bash items.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `web_fetch` dead air. Agent calls `web_fetch` on a slow URL; before this patch the channel shows nothing for the full fetch duration. After the patch, a single `"Fetching web page…"` progress line appears ~1 s in and stays until the result lands.
- Real environment tested: Node 22.22.2 / Linux 6.8.0-111-generic / live network. Slow path = `https://httpbin.org/delay/3` (3 s server-side hold). Fast path = `https://example.com/` (~400 ms total). The harness drives the exact tool execution code path (`createWebFetchTool().execute(...)`); the full agent → channel-runtime chain to a live bot turn was not exercised (see "What was not tested").
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  Two complementary pillars — the in-tree controlled test plus a live-network harness:

  ```
  # (a) controlled / committed
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-fetch.progress.test.ts --run
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts --run

  # (b) live network (proof harness, not committed; lives under .local/)
  PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \
    node --import tsx .local/c2-tool-progress-web-fetch/20260526T160532Z/run-proof.mjs
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  ```
  --- web_fetch real behavior proof: slow ---
  url=https://httpbin.org/delay/3
  total=4203ms emitCount=1
    t+ 1002ms  Fetching web page…
  result-preview: {  "url": "https://httpbin.org/delay/3", ...

  --- web_fetch real behavior proof: fast ---
  url=https://example.com/
  total=406ms emitCount=0
  result-preview: {  "url": "https://example.com/", ...

  === verdict: PASS ===
    slow path observed (1 emit, generic text, t+1s±0.5s): yes
    slow path duplicate emits: no
    slow completion (real fetch returned with body): yes
    fast path spam (any emit): no
    privacy (no URL/host/query in emit text): yes
  ```

- Observed result after fix: slow real network → exactly one emit at `t+1002 ms` with literal text `"Fetching web page…"`, fetch completes normally at `t+4203 ms` with body. Fast real network → zero emits, fetch completes at `t+406 ms`. No duplicate, no late emit after completion. Result-shape continuity (URL / finalUrl / status / contentType / extractor / text / fetchedAt / tookMs) is covered by the unchanged `web-fetch.{cf-markdown,provider-fallback,ssrf,visibility}.test.ts` suite (81 tests, all green).
- What was not tested: live channel-side render to a Telegram/Discord/Slack bot. The chain to the channel draft line is proven by static read against unchanged `channel-streaming.ts:buildChannelProgressDraftLine` plus the existing `channel-streaming.test.ts` regression that exercises the `progressText` fallback via exec items.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live-network half depends on httpbin.org availability and a typical `/delay/3` response within ~3.5 s. The harness asserts `firstEmitMs ∈ [800, 2500]` to absorb local clock jitter under load.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-patch on the same SHA, the slow-path emit count is exactly 0 (no `onUpdate` wiring existed in `web-fetch.ts`); channel renderers had no `progressText` to draw, so users saw a static "web_fetch" label for the full 3 s. Post-patch: 0 emits during the silent gap → 1 emit ~1 s in.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-fetch.progress.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-fetch.cf-markdown.test.ts \
                            src/agents/tools/web-fetch.provider-fallback.test.ts \
                            src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ssrf.test.ts \
                            src/agents/tools/web-fetch-visibility.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
                            src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental \
  --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental \
  --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

Result summary (all green): web-fetch.progress 10 / handlers.tools 90 / channel-streaming 22 / sibling web-fetch 81 / agent-runner-execution + followup-runner 190. `tsgo:core` and `tsgo:test` exit 0.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/tools/web-fetch.progress.test.ts` (new, 5 cases × 2 lanes): slow path single emit; fast path zero emit; error cleanup (snapshot-then-stable); fail-open onUpdate; privacy contract (URL/auth/cookies absent from emit text).
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts` (extended, 4 new cases under `handleToolExecutionUpdate non-exec progress`): allow-listed `web_fetch` populates `progressText`; cap applied; **non-allow-list non-exec tool no leak** (`does not surface progressText for non-allow-listed non-exec tools`); exec command-output regression.

What failed before this fix, if known?

No upstream test failure — the silent-gap UX was a missing-emit, not a broken assertion. New tests are net-additive.

If no test was added, why not? — N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **Yes.** A single new progress line appears in channel drafts during slow `web_fetch` calls (≥1 s). Tool result / error / cache / timeout semantics unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **No.** No new config keys, env vars, defaults, or migrations.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) — **No** for security / auth / secrets. The emit payload is a fixed literal with no URL, query, header, cookie, or body content. SSRF guard, timeout, redirects, abort, retry, and cache are untouched. (User-visible behavior — the new progress line — is captured under row 1.)

What is the highest-risk area? — Channel-side rendering of the new `progressText` field on the `kind:"tool"` item. Channel renderers already consume this field for exec command items, so the integration risk is whether the channel draft line renders the same when the source is a non-exec item.

How is that risk mitigated? — The runtime emits `progressText` on the same `kind:"tool"` item shape and via the existing `buildChannelProgressDraftLine` fallback. No channel adapter source was modified. The 22-test `channel-streaming.test.ts` regression covers the fallback. `extractLiveNonExecProgressText` reuses `LIVE_EXEC_OUTPUT_MAX_CHARS = 8000` so a misbehaving allow-listed tool cannot flush a large body into a draft.

## Current review state

What is the next action? — Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? — Awaiting CI green and maintainer review. No outstanding author work.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? — None yet (first review round).
target.author: masatohoshino

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
